title: senna insularis
intro: Senna insularis, also known as the insular senna, is a plant species native to the island of Réunion in the Indian Ocean. It is a member of the legume family and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative due to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Senna insularis include its ability to relieve constipation, as well as its potential to help manage symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Senna insularis is often prepared as a herbal tea or dried pod, and it can also be used in combination with other herbal remedies to enhance its effects.
